creator: marco.broeder@posteo.eu
text:
Did you actually read everything i wrote? Looks like you did not.

And looking at your patch I have the impression you did not look carefully 
enough at the -whole- Makefile. You try to add things which are already there 
and which are working fine. See my previous email.

> 1 - The '.include <bsd.port.options.mk>', is not necessary, the use of
> OPTIONS is already being done in full interaction with the User.

Why was it then introduced? Why is it mentioned in Porters Handbook as one 
recommended way to handle options, if it is not necessary? Sounds 
unreasonable.

> 2- 'Added INSTALLS_OMF to Makefile' this is necessary to ports that
> installing OMF. See in many ports that already exist.

It was and is already there! See my previous email.

> A tip, always run the command: portlint -ca
> This command is your friend.

I always do that! No errors or warnings reported. Everything is fine.

> 3- 'Changed form how install docs'  was added to more accurately and
> installation of synthetic documents without the need to describe them
> in pkg-plist.

See my previous email and my PR ports/139608 .

> The installation of the documentation should be a standard for the
> port where the user only one run: make install.

It already was and is!

> If the user does not need the documentation, he should run the command:
> make install -DNOPORTDOCS
> This is the standard around the port.

I know. And it already worked and works this way.

> 
> 4- Use of NLS. You know the porters-handbook? It is an excellent
> manual that describes the correct way of using gettext:

I know that and it is correctly used. See my previous email.

Well, it does not matter at all. There already is an updated patch in my PR 
and the way I like to maintain the port.

Thanks!

-- 
Regards,
Marco Bröder <marco.broeder@gmx.eu>
OpenPGP key fingerprint: 5615 106E 031A F3D3 64CC 0F9E 4DCE 6524 F595 082F
